title: JECP Hub Discovery Document
description: Schema for /.well-known/agent-guide.json — Hub-published discovery + conformance manifest. See spec 05-discovery §4.5.

$schema.description: Schema URI — clients SHOULD validate this document against it.

version.description: JECP spec version this Hub claims conformance with (semver, e.g., '1.0.2').

last_updated.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the last modification of this document. Documents older than 7 days SHOULD be treated as stale by clients.

vendor_prefix.description: Lowercase 2-8 character vendor identifier used in this Hub's agent_id / api_key / provider_id strings (per 02-authentication §3.1).

hub_operator.description: Legal entity operating this Hub (e.g., 'Tufe Company Inc.'). Optional.

hub_url.description: Origin URL of this Hub (HTTPS required).

endpoints.description: Concrete endpoint URLs; complements `register_endpoint`. The canonical execution endpoint is `invoke`. `invoke_legacy_alias` (if present) points to the deprecated `/v1/jecp` path retained through v1.x.

supported_capabilities.description: List of capability names (e.g., 'content-factory') or fully-qualified third-party capabilities (e.g., 'jobdonebot/streaming-test').

conformance_levels.description: Conformance level claims. Verifiable via the Phase 2 conformance harness (`docker run ghcr.io/jecpdev/conformance:v1.x --target <hub_url>`).

replay_cache_mode.description: Implementation strategy for the Provenance v2 nonce replay cache (see 02-authentication §5.2.1).

provenance_versions_supported.description: Which Provenance hash schemes the Hub verifies. v1 sunset 2026-11-01; new Hubs SHOULD ship v2 only.

register_endpoint.description: URL where new Agents register to obtain agent_id + api_key.

support.description: Email or URL for general support.

security.description: Email for vulnerability disclosure (often pairs with `/.well-known/security.txt`).

spec_version_pinned.description: Exact spec patch this Hub is tested against (e.g., '1.0.2').

rate_limits_default_rpm.description: Default per-tier rate limits (requests per minute) — informational for capacity planning.

currencies_accepted.description: ISO 4217 + crypto extension codes the Hub accepts (e.g., ['USD','USDC']).

regions.description: Fly.io / cloud regions this Hub serves from. Single-region Hubs disclose this honestly; multi-region Hubs list all primaries.

trust_policy.description: Open object describing the Hub's Trust Tier policy and Provenance enforcement.

extensions.description: Open object for vendor-specific fields. Keys SHOULD use vendor namespace (e.g., 'x-jdb-foo').
